question:
Will the price of Ethereum be greater than $4,400 on October 23?

Description: This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

evidences.title: ETH Up or Down Daily Predictions & Odds 2026 | Polymarket
evidences.content: This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT Jul 4 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Jul 5 '26 12:00 ET candle. This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT Jul 4 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Jul 5 '26 12:00 ET candle. If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50. The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar. evidences.title: Binance API — python-binance 0.2.0 documentation
evidences.content: Message Format ``` {"e": "kline", # event type "E": 1499404907056, # event time "s": "ETHBTC", # symbol "k":{"t": 1499404860000, # start time of this bar "T": 1499404919999, # end time of this bar "s": "ETHBTC", # symbol "i": "1m", # interval "f": 77462, # first trade id "L": 77465, # last trade id "o":"0.10278577", # open "c":"0.10278645", # close "h":"0.10278712", # high "l":"0.10278518", # low "v":"17.47929838", # volume "n": 4, # number of trades "x": false, # whether this bar is final "q":"1.79662878", # quote volume "V":"2.34879839", # volume of active buy "Q":"0.24142166", # quote volume of active buy "B":"13279784.01349473" # can be ignored}} ``` [...] options\_index\_price\_socket()(_modules/binance/ws/streams.html#BinanceSocketManager.options_index_price_socket) : Subscribe to an options index price stream. Underlying (e.g., ETHUSDT, BTCUSDT) index stream for all symbols. Updates every 1000ms. URL PATH: /market Stream Name: !index@arr API Reference: Response is an array with index price for all underlying assets: - Event type: ‘indexPrice’ - Event time - Underlying symbol (e.g., ‘ETHUSDT’, ‘BTCUSDT’) - Index price options\_kline\_socket(symbol: str, interval='1m')(_mo...

evidences.content: # binance\_klines: Get kline/candlestick data from Binance In daroczig/binancer: API Client to 'Binance' View source: R/binance.R | | | --- | | binance\_klines | R Documentation | Get kline/candlestick data from BinanceDescription Get kline/candlestick data from Binance Usage ``` binance_klines( symbol, interval = c("1m", "3m", "5m", "15m", "30m", "1h", "2h", "4h", "6h", "8h", "12h", "1d", "3d", "1w", "1M"), limit, start_time, end_time ) ``` Arguments | | | --- | | `symbol` | string | | `interval` | enum | | `limit` | optional int | | `start_time` | optional POSIX timestamp | | `end_time` | optional POSIX timestamp | Value `data.table` with open-high-low-close values Examples ``` ## Not run: binance_klines('ETHUSDT') binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', limit = 247) [...] binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', limit = 247) binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', start_time = '2018-01-01', end_time = '2018-01-08') ## End(Not run) ``` daroczig/binancer documentation built on March 5, 2024, 4:28 a.m. Related to binance\_klines in daroczig/binancer... daroczig/binancer index README.md R Package Documentation rdrr.io home R language documentation Run R code online Browse...

evidences.title: Kline Candlestick Data | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: Binance Logo Binance Logo # Kline/Candlestick Data ## API Description​ Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months ## HTTP Request​ GET `/dapi/v1/klines` `/dapi/v1/klines` ## Request Weight​ based on parameter `LIMIT` `LIMIT` | LIMIT | weight | --- | | [1,100) | 1 | | [100, 500) | 2 | | [500, 1000] | 5 | 1000 | 10 ## Request Parameters​ | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | After CM migration, accepts both CM and UM symbols. | | interval | ENUM | YES | | | startTime | LONG | NO | | | endTime | LONG | NO | | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1500. | [...] `startTime` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `limit` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` ## Response Example​ `[ [ 1591258320000, // Open time "9640.7", // Open "9642.4", // High "9640.6", // Low "9642.0", // Close (or latest price) "206", // Volume 1591258379999, // Close time "2.13660389", // Base asset volume 48, // Number of trades "119", // Taker buy volume "1.23424865", //...

evidences.title: Ethereum (ETH) Price Prediction 2026 2027 2028 - 2040 - Changelly
evidences.content: ## FAQ ### What Is the Prediction for Ethereum in 2026? Analyst projections range widely: Benzinga’s aggregated data places ETH between $3,000 and $6,000 in bullish conditions. InvestingHaven suggests a $2,500–$5,500 band. Citi has cited $4,500, while Standard Chartered has referenced $7,500 under stronger institutional participation. Machine-learning models estimate an average near $4,359. ### How Much Will 1 Ethereum Be Worth in 2030? Analyst projections range widely. Finder’s expert panel projects a long-term price of $10,000+ for ETH by 2030, as adoption and staking participation expand. Other 2030 projections span a huge range: [...] Institutional adoption. Spot Ethereum ETFs launched in 2024 expanded institutional access. ETF inflows and outflows now function as an observable demand indicator. Protocol upgrades.Major upgrades—including The Merge (2022), Shapella (2023), Dencun (2024), and Pectra (2025)—have reshaped Ethereum’s technical and economic design. Successful upgrades typically strengthen long-term confidence. Competition from other Layer-1 networks. Ethereum competes with alternative smart contract platforms for developers, capital, and user activity. Shifts in n...

evidences.title: Ethereum Analyst ratings price targets 2026 - Bitcoin Foundation
evidences.content: For market readers following Ethereum price prediction July 2026, this split shows why ETH forecasts remain wide: the network is structurally strong, but the token still needs better liquidity and stronger demand to break higher. The ETH price target analyst ratings 2026 picture therefore looks cautiously constructive, but not cleanly bullish until ETH shows better momentum against Bitcoin and broader altcoin liquidity improves. ### Institutional Outlook on Ethereum While institutional Ethereum views are becoming a major driver of how investment professionals perceive the Ethereum market forecast, big banks and large financial institutions are adjusting their views on the potential Ethereum price based on regulatory developments (or lack thereof) and macroeconomic trends. [...] A negative Ethereum price prediction for 2026 will be heavily affected by both general economic stresses and the weakness of the markets. It has become apparent from recent data that ETH has already lost the earlier $2,050–$2,150 area, making the $1,500–$1,600 zone the new short-term level to watch. evidences.title: Ethereum Forecast 2026: Foundation in Crisis, 70% Below ...
evidences.content: > ## Ethereum Price Prediction 2026 — Key Notes > > Ethereum Price Prediction H2 2026: The path depends on three questions: Does the Glamsterdam upgrade deliver and rebuild the technical investment narrative? Do ETF flows reverse as the macro environment shifts? And can the Ethereum ecosystem close the development funding gap left by the EF's restructuring without losing research momentum? > + BASE CASE ~ 45% ($2,500 – $4,500): Glamsterdam delivers in Q3 2026, the Ethereum Foundation restructuring stabilises, and ETF outflows moderate. ETH recovers toward the $2,500–$4,500 range — consistent with Standard Chartered's $4,000 year-end target, Fundstrat's internal $4,500, and Citi's $3,175 base case. The macro environment remains challenging (hawkish Fed) but not deteriorating further. [...] ### January 2024: Bitcoin Spot ETF Approval — $2,400 The January 10, 2024 Bitcoin ETF approval signalled regulators were opening to crypto via traditional finance, with ETH at ~$2,400. Spot Ethereum ETFs followed, creating a new institutional demand channel for ETH through pension, insurance, and wealth management mandates. ### August 25, 2025: All-Time High — $4,946 Ethereum reached $4,946 on...

evidences.title: VanEck Crypto Monthly Recap for October 2023 | VanEck
evidences.content: Bitcoin surged by the most since January, +28% in October, as digital assets outpaced the Nasdaq (-2%) for the second month in a row. Large-caps (+21%) again beat small-caps (+4%), even as the ETH-to-BTC ratio fell below the levels set during the 3AC liquidation in June 2022. Demand for on-chain leverage remained somewhat muted, with DeFi TVL growth merely keeping pace with the incremental rise in ETH prices, hardly an impressive feat. Rather, the activity came from the regulated space: flows into Bitcoin ETNs and ETFs globally reached $412M in the month compared to $630M total YTD, while the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME’s) share of Bitcoin futures activity reached record levels (23%). We attribute this surge to astute investors positioning themselves ahead of the impending wave of [...] The Ethereum ecosystem, including its L2 smart contract platforms, was again front and center in the October news cycle. On October 11, Ethereum saw its validator queue empty, indicating that there was no waiting line for new entities to stake ETH and become a validator for Ethereum. Although more than 14k validators joined the network in October, demand was insufficient to create a queue, a...

evidences.title: November Economic Calendar: Key economic events in November that could impact crypto
evidences.content: 4 November ### Why investors care The upgrade will merge Polkadot’s core services into a single chain, aiming to make the network easier to use, improve interoperability, and strengthen its role as a key ecosystem hub. This major technical and strategic upgrade has the potential to drive adoption and lift market sentiment. ### US unemployment data 7 November ### Why investors care Whether data will be released on time amid the ongoing US government shutdown is uncertain. With the Fed citing early stress signals in the jobs sector, investors will be watching closely to gauge the pace of future rate cuts. September’s data was missing, but estimates and private sources suggest the unemployment rate held around 4.34-4.35%, slightly above August’s 4.3%. [...] The US government shutdown left most economic data unavailable during October, forcing the Fed to call in favours with furloughed staff to release the Consumer Price Index (CPI) numbers on 23 October. Lower-than-expected inflation injected some positive sentiment into markets, boosting expectations of an interest rate cut, which the Fed confirmed later in the month with a 0.25% reduction. Despite the volatility, Bitcoin finished...

evidences.title: ETH ETF Flows, Treasury Buys, and the Crypto Rally
evidences.content: # ETH ETF Flows, Treasury Buys, and the Crypto Rally ## Galaxy 35900 subscribers 7 likes ### Description 288 views Posted: 15 Aug 2025 For this week’s Trading Take, Beimnet Abebe (Galaxy Trading) joins Alex Thorn (Galaxy Research) to break down Ethereum’s recent rally, including record ETF inflows and billions in treasury company purchases. They discuss how elevated funding rates, derivatives positioning, and a wave of institutional demand are fueling the move, and what could happen if ETH breaks its all-time high. Beimnet also shares his outlook for Bitcoin as it approaches price discovery, explains why risk appetite is back across markets, and highlights the macro conditions driving asset prices higher. This episode was recorded on Wednesday, August 13, 2025. evidences.title: Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for ...
evidences.content: Candle: H1 closes green (+$200) Action: Buy at $60,000 SL: $59,800 (low of prior candle, $200 risk) TP: $60,400 (1:2 ratio, $400 profit) Outcome: Price hits $60,400, yielding profit; or hits $59,800, incurring a small loss. Trade 2: ETH/USDT (H1, Afternoon Session) Location: London, UK (12:00 BST) Candle: H1 closes red (-$150) Action: Short at $59,500 SL: $59,650 (high of prior candle, $150 risk) TP: $59,200 (1:2 ratio, $300 profit) Outcome: Price drops to $59,200 for profit; or rises to $59,650, triggering a loss. Why This Strategy Shines Simplicity: No need for complex indicators—just follow candlestick colors. Flexibility: Trade at times that suit your local schedule, aligned with global market sessions. [...] What Is the Green/Red Candle Strategy? The Green/Red Candle Strategy uses the color of candlesticks on a 1-hour (H1) or 15-minute (M15) chart to determine whether to buy or sell. It’s designed for cryptocurrency pairs with strong liquidity and volatility, such as BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, or BNB/USDT, and can be implemented on platforms like Binance, Bybit, or KuCoin. Traders select three daily trading times based on their local time zone, aligning with active market sessions...

evidences.title: Binance Market Data - Amberdata
evidences.content: We offer order book data across all four of our market data sections: spot, swaps, futures and options. In addition to the full order book, we also have snapshots of the order books for the same four sections. These snapshots are 1-minute looks at the order books. If you don’t need the deepest granularity of the full order book, the snapshot endpoints are the next best thing and enable you to see what happened at a higher level for any pair at any time on Binance. We offer Order book snapshots and order book events with granularity from one minute to one day. Order book events with every latest order book bid and ask on Binance. Our order book data is extremely granular and includes every ‘flick’ of a bid or ask for any pair on Binance. [...] We collect Order Book Snapshots via Binance’s REST API: the snapshot is a one minute snapshot. Every minute we get the full order book, full depth, as much as Binance provides. We offer real-time streaming order book data via Websocket subscriptions for all four order book types; spot, options, futures and swaps. We also offer FIX protocol. We get our order book data directly from Binance. Our Order Book endpoints are available via REST API...
